claimText: Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement involves a decentralized approach for the bilateral trading of Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes (ITMOs).
evidenceExcerpt: Article 6.2 involves decentralized bilateral trading of Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes (ITMOs) with corresponding NDC adjustments.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing the decentralized nature of Article 6.2 from the centralized Article 6.4 mechanism.

claimText: Article 6.4 of the Paris Agreement establishes a centralized global carbon market known as the Paris Agreement Crediting Mechanism (PACM), which succeeds the Kyoto Protocol's Clean Development Mechanism (CDM).
evidenceExcerpt: Article 6.4 establishes a centralized global carbon market (PACM), succeeding the Kyoto Protocol's Clean Development Mechanism (CDM).
examUse: Correct-pair or comparison question on the evolution of international carbon markets from Kyoto to Paris.

claimText: Article 6.8 of the Paris Agreement focuses exclusively on non-market approaches and does not involve the trading of emission reductions.
evidenceExcerpt: Article 6.8 focuses on non-market approaches with no trading of emission reductions.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the scope and limitations of Article 6 mechanisms.
distractorHooks: Allows bilateral trading of emission reductions

claimText: The National Designated Authority (NDA) responsible for authorizing projects under Article 6.4 is a 21-member committee headed by the Secretary of the Environment Ministry.
evidenceExcerpt: NDA is a 21-member committee headed by the Secretary, Environment Ministry, authorizing projects under Article 6.4.
examUse: Institution-feature question on the domestic regulatory framework for international carbon markets in India.
distractorHooks: Headed by the Minister of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

claimText: The Paris Agreement mandates the allocation of 5 percent of the proceeds from Article 6.4 transactions to the Global Adaptation Fund.
evidenceExcerpt: Paris Agreement allocates 5% of proceeds from Article 6.4 transactions to the Global Adaptation Fund
examUse: Statement-based question on climate finance mechanisms and transaction levies under the Paris Agreement.
distractorHooks: Allocates proceeds to the Green Climate Fund

claimText: The Paris Agreement restricts the use of legacy carbon credits to those generated post-2013, whereas the Kyoto Protocol allowed the use of older credits.
evidenceExcerpt: Paris Agreement restricts legacy credit use to post-2013 credits, unlike Kyoto which allowed older credits.
examUse: Comparison question evaluating the transition rules for carbon credits between the Kyoto Protocol and the Paris Agreement.
distractorHooks: Bans the use of all legacy credits
Kyoto Protocol restricted the use of older credits
